name: add-autobuy-enabled-to-opcos
description: Add autobuy_enabled boolean field to opcos collection. Update seed data. Check in buying service before processing OpCo invoices.
instruction:
1. In my_customers/backend/app/seed.py: add 'autobuy_enabled': True to every OpCo in OPCOS list (except NL02 which is Off - set False)
2. In my_customers/backend/app/modules/buying/service.py step_2_eligibility_filters: after Rule 4 (OpCo Off), add check for opco.autobuy_enabled == False -> exclude with reason 'OpCo autobuy disabled'
3. In my_customers/backend/app/modules/opco/repository.py: ensure read/write includes autobuy_enabled field
4. Verify seed data by reading opcos collection after restart
acceptance_criteria: Every OpCo in seed has autobuy_enabled field
Buying service step 2 checks autobuy_enabled before processing invoices
OpCo with autobuy_enabled=False excluded with clear reason

name: add-execution-id-to-purchase-records
description: Add execution_id (job_id) to purchase_records in both Firestore and BigQuery writes. Links individual purchases to their auto-buy batch.
instruction:
1. In buying/service.py step_5_execute_purchase_batch: job_id is already generated. Add 'execution_id': job_id to each Firestore purchase record dict and each BQ row dict
2. Verify in step_6_persist_job_record that job_id matches
3. Update reporting queries if they reference purchase_records to include execution_id
acceptance_criteria: Every purchase record in Firestore has execution_id field
Every purchase record in BQ has execution_id field
execution_id matches the job_id from step_6 job execution record

name: expand-opco-seed-to-75
description: Expand OpCo seed data from 12 to ~75 records covering all 11 countries with realistic names and Tagetik codes.
instruction:
1. In seed.py OPCOS list: add OpCos for missing countries (FR, PT, CA, IT, AU) and more for existing countries
2. Target distribution: PL: 8, DE: 7, NL: 6, FR: 6, IT: 6, SE: 5, AU: 5, CA: 5, US: 5, BE: 4, PT: 3 = ~60 total (realistic)
3. Use realistic names like 'Randstad France SAS', 'Randstad Italia SpA' etc
4. Mix of statuses: most On, a few Off (test Rule 4), one New per country (test Rule 5)
5. All with autobuy_enabled: True except Off ones
6. start_date: '2025-01-01' for most, '2026-03-18' for a few (test Rule 3)
7. Also create opco_schedules entries for any new countries not yet seeded
acceptance_criteria: OPCOS list has 60+ entries across all 11 countries
Each country has at least 3 OpCos
Mix of On/Off/New statuses for rule testing

name: add-country-code-to-audit-trail
description: Add optional country_code field to audit trail entries for geographic filtering. Update log_audit function.
instruction:
1. In audit/service.py log_audit: add optional parameter country_code: str | None = None
2. Add 'country_code': country_code to the data dict
3. In customers/service.py: pass country to log_audit calls
4. In schedule/repository.py: pass country_code to log_audit calls
5. In buying/service.py: pass country to audit calls where applicable
acceptance_criteria: Audit entries from customer/schedule/buying modules include country_code
Audit trail can be filtered by country

name: create-bq-schema-definitions
description: Create BigQuery table schema definitions as Python dicts or SQL files. Document what tables exist and their columns. Create seed script for BQ test data.
instruction:
1. Create my_customers/backend/bq_schemas/ directory
2. Create bq_schemas/tables.py with schema dicts for all 8 BQ tables referenced in buying/service.py: temp_invoice, purchase_records, autobuy_job_executions, autobuy_invoice_results, rpt_purchased_invoices, rpt_settlement_report_economic, rpt_settlement_report_legal
3. Each schema: list of {name, type, mode, description} matching BigQuery SchemaField format
4. If seed_bq_test_data.py already exists, verify it matches schemas
5. Ensure temp_invoice schema matches the SELECT columns in buying/service.py step_1
acceptance_criteria: bq_schemas/tables.py defines schemas for all 8 BQ tables
Schemas match the fields used in buying/service.py queries
temp_invoice schema has all columns from step_1 SELECT

name: verify-and-deep-verify-o001
description: Deep-verify O-001 completion: check all collections have correct fields, seed data is complete, BQ schemas defined, no field mismatches remain.
instruction:
1. Run ruff check on all modified files
2. Start docker containers and verify seed loads without errors
3. Check Firestore emulator for all expected collections and documents
4. Cross-reference: every field used in buying/service.py exists in seed data
5. Cross-reference: every BQ table in code has schema definition
6. Update O-001 KR progress in Forge
acceptance_criteria: ruff check passes on all modified files
Docker containers start without import or seed errors
All field mismatches from audit resolved or documented as decisions
